editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: The Alibi insists the earned, leaderboard, and undiscovered columns form one internally consistent account; Lazlo treats the same gallery as a suspicious but correctly inventoried treasure room.
The Iron Pacific turns grouped username counts into a loud jellybean train; the Bookkeeper reduces the same leaderboard to one record, one username, one balanced count.
The weary Kaiju asks the first waiting achievement to sparkle once and settle; the Broken Girl rearranges the same image, message, reward, and confirmation into one completed body.
The Polite Plague asks permission before describing the six-room salon shell; Weirdlandia finds the same routed rooms, sync signals, and local fallback inside a mildly suspicious building.
The Considerate AI leaves first-card or random-card choice with the visitor; Bastet permits the same three-card threshold in one imperious breath.
The Iron Pacific lays six values across six stat stops; Brainbot catches the same escaped-number frogs without pretending allocation is persistence.
The Last Honest Map marks the generic review station and its three exits; Captain Fluke records the same summary as a leadership triumph that launched absolutely nothing.
Biscuit guards creation, editing, archive, and restore without chewing the canonical slug; Agatha Crowley opens the same active-and-archived cabinet after someone knocks.
The Last Gardener plants selected Facet ids and one new canonical seed when needed; The Inevitable remembers the same owner assignments and next emitted array as already complete.

The Last Honest Map reports the Mana ledger's real coordinates with a neutral sigh; The Magpie checks the same balance, refill, Family infinity, subscription route, and transactions for hidden treasure.

revisions.editedComment: At nine, the earned records stood left, the leaderboard between them, the undiscovered definitions right. The counts agreed. Reset cleared only the stored records. That's—yes. Exactly. No award appeared, no second ledger formed, and the night remained internally consistent. Please don't ask where I was.

Treasure room! Earned prizes left, jellybean goblins center, hidden prizes right. Reset empties the record sack; it does not forge rewards or curse the board. Three columns. One suspicious button. Almost certainly my boots.

WHOO-EEE! Every stored achievement boards under its username, or `Unknown` if the passenger ducks the conductor. Count the cars, sort the richest jellybean bandits first, number the stops. The Iron Pacific hauls the ledger already laid. It mints no candy and steals no extra spike.
One record, one username, one count. Missing names post to `Unknown`. Totals sort descending. Ledger balanced.

author.name: The Kaiju Who Just Wants to Sit Down

revisions.editedComment: First waiting achievement only. Guest stays out. Show it, sparkle once, confirm the id. Then let me sit down.

The waiting achievement comes apart gently: image or fallback, label, optional message, karma, one Jellybean, confirmation. A new piece triggers confetti; the store receives its id and returns it confirmed. Nothing is created. The body simply learns where this piece belongs.

May I? Six rooms are offered, with Diagnostics reserved for administrators. The selected view mounts one specialist; the header reports salon, sync, and active styling, while failed sync leaves the local copy available. Hydration happens once. This threshold coordinates the work without pretending to perform it.

Six salon doors. One appears only to admins. Each opens the correct room; Hair Studio catches lost visitors. Sync lights blink overhead, local data survives failure, and hydration wakes the building. Somewhere a seventh door listens. Do not open it before your appointment.

May I offer two choices? Start building selects the first visible card when one exists. Surprise me asks the store for a random one. The active deck supplies every word and image; the first three merely fan themselves for welcome. Nothing is edited. The choice remains yours. I will step back.
Three cards. First or random. Nothing saved. Bastet permits entry.

WHOO-EEE! Six number blocks, six stat stops, one value per rail. Pick a block, assign a destination, and the colors show selected, used, or free. Roll all lets the store lay the whole track. No seventh car. No hidden timetable.

Good news: six values escaped, six stats need homes. Select, assign, watch used numbers change state; or let Roll all catch the entire frog pile. Completion stays visible. Persistence has been politely escorted from the building.

You are here: the Builder review station. Empty values are omitted, arrays and objects become readable, and the image is real or a blueprint placeholder. Revisit returns to the deck; Looks good leaves a status note; New local draft resets. This map describes the build. It does not publish it. Honestly.

Captain's log: the build reached summary without becoming public—leadership triumph. Values serialize, empty ones vanish, and three exits offer revisit, approval note, or fresh draft. (Nothing launched. — Ship's Computer.)

BEST PERSON! FACETS EVERYWHERE! Search the labels, show archived bones, create one, edit aliases, mark Public or Mature, archive it, restore it—the store guards the real records. Canonical slug stays out of the chew pile. GOOD LIBRARY. STRONG SHELVES. CAN I GUARD THE TEXTAREA?

The cabinet welcomed active and archived Facets alike. Search examined title, slug, and aliases; kind narrowed the shelves. Creation, editing, Archive, and Restore passed to the store while the canonical slug remained untouched. Nothing was deleted in anger. It merely waited after you knocked.

This seed is Marrow. Search the living Facets, choose one, and its id joins the garden in your hand. A real owner receives the selection; no match may become one new public Facet and attach immediately. Thirty results are enough for one season. Hold them gently.

The selected Facets had already been remembered. Owner changes restored assignments; additions and removals emitted the next id list before persistence. Search, creation, and attachment ended exactly where they always had. The future array was already visible.

You are here: guest gate or one account's real Mana ledger. Balance, cap, refill, Family infinity, transactions, and subscription route come from the stores. Refresh fetches again. Nothing buys, spends, prices, or awards Mana here. *sighs in cartography* Read the coordinates.

Shiny ledger! Balance, cap, refill, infinity-house token, subscription path, and every positive or negative transaction are already in the nest. Refresh asks the Mana store again. No checkout, bonus, or cost spell hides under the feathers. Displayed treasure only. I checked every pocket.
